worktree.targetHost: dirty before this gate; no target-host files were edited by this gate

conclusion: codex-router is effective when work enters DesktopHostClient/runDesktopTask and VCPChat exposes a real CodexRouterHost entry, but the router is not yet a mandatory/global governance layer for all Codex or VCPChat user-visible actions.

validatedInputs.summary: Proves codex-router changes execution posture when the call enters the router path; direct CLI remains the unrouted baseline.
Proves VCPChat native host can load codex-router, build a host bundle, select policy/model/tool posture, and execute through safe stub bindings.
Historical live Electron addendum reports renderer control-path smoke for inspect/run/resume, with run/resume intentionally preflight-blocked to avoid side effects.
Historical acceptance document records live inspect/run/resume integration surface acceptance.
Historical closeout records the first production-oriented native host embedding path as implemented and landed.

bypassPaths.path: normal VCPChat send button / enter-key chat send
bypassPaths.reason: The normal chat send path calls chatManager.handleSendMessage and is separate from window.codexRouterHostClient.run.
Direct CLI execution does not automatically load codex-router routing-policy.yaml or enter DesktopHostClient/runDesktopTask.

evidence: Codex Desktop runtime inspection found packaged bootstrap/main bundle but no editable source entry that loads this router.
bypassPaths.reason: The official packaged Desktop runtime does not, by itself, consume this repository's router policy.
Only DesktopRemote requests with command CodexRouterHost enter the router host handler; other DesktopRemote commands remain normal desktop remote actions.

mandatoryGovernanceCriteria.criterion: All in-scope user-visible Codex actions enter DesktopHostClient/runDesktopTask or an equivalent enforced router wrapper.
evidence: normal VCPChat chat send path bypasses window.codexRouterHostClient

mandatoryGovernanceCriteria.criterion: Direct CLI/Desktop usage is either wrapped, disabled for governed workflows, or explicitly labeled unmanaged.
evidence: direct CLI remains the unrouted baseline

mandatoryGovernanceCriteria.criterion: A production-like VCPChat entry can execute through codex-router.
Live Electron/HTTP smoke proves the exact governed production action path.
evidence: historical live Electron renderer control-path smoke exists
current gate did not run a fresh live Electron or HTTP smoke

decision.recommendedAcceptanceLabel: router available; governance not mandatory yet

nextGates.why: Mandatory governance needs a declared scope before code enforcement can be judged.

nextGates.gate: route selected VCPChat Codex action through codexRouterHostClient
nextGates.why: The normal chat send path currently bypasses the router.

nextGates.gate: add production-path smoke for the selected route
nextGates.why: A fresh live Electron or loopback HTTP smoke should prove the exact action path that users will exercise.

nextGates.gate: document direct CLI/Desktop as unmanaged unless codex-cli-host or another wrapper is active
nextGates.why: Direct tools do not become governed merely because codex-router exists.
